mnemonics: Diffie-Hellman: Ducks (Diffie) and Hens (Hellman) meet publicly to create a SECRET egg (key). They mix their own private colors (private keys) with a public color (public parameters) to create a shared secret color no one else can see.
keyPoints: Diffie-Hellman allows two parties to establish a shared secret key over an insecure channel without any prior communication.
The security of Diffie-Hellman relies on the computational difficulty of the Discrete Logarithm Problem (DLP).
Man-in-the-Middle (MITM) attacks are a significant vulnerability for basic Diffie-Hellman, which is why authenticated versions (like STS) are used.
commonMistakes: Assuming Diffie-Hellman provides authentication; it only provides key establishment and is vulnerable to MITM attacks without additional authentication steps.
Confusing the key exchange protocol (Diffie-Hellman) with the encryption algorithm (like AES) that will use the generated key.
Using weak or predictable private keys (a, b), which compromises the entire security of the exchanged key.
